//<!--
show\_header('Configuration Options');
show\_index();
//-->

The Options window shows configuration values for the current open project, or the global defaults if no projects are open.

Note: Many Site3D project settings can be set in a more user friendly way from specific property windows, for example the [Project Options Window](project-options.htm) and the [Section View Display Properties Window](longsection-display-settings.htm).

To access the **Options** window click the **Tools->Options...** menu option.

[Screenshot: options-dlg.png - Configuration Options](images/options-dlg.png)

### Project or Global settings

Choose from the drop-down menu whether to edit settings for a particular project, or your global settings. If you have one or more projects open, you can choose which one to edit the configuration for.

The **Global** settings are the default settings that will be used the next time a new project is created. Changing the global settings will generally have no affect on an existing project.

### Enterprise settings

Your entire organisation can use a single configuration file so that you all use the same settings.

To achieve this is quite straight forward; one user configures their global settings the way that is wanted for the whole organisation, then their configuration file is copied to a shared folder that everyone can access (for instance, on a network drive).

Here is the step-by-step process to do it:

* Start Site3D (without opening a project).
* Select the Tools->Options menu option. This will display the Options window. Make sure the **Global** option is selected on the Project drop-down.
* Configure all the settings as desired for your organisation.
* Close Site3D. (The settings are only saved when you close Site3D).
* In Windows Explorer, enter **%appdata%\Site3D** into the address bar. This will show the folder where the configuration file is saved for the Windows user. (For example, it will take you to "C:\Users\*UserName*\AppData\Roaming\Site3D", where "UserName" is the current user account name)
* Copy the file **Site3D-user.ini** to the shared folder location. (For example, you might have a network drive folder called "P:\Share\Site3D"
* In the shared folder, rename the configuration file from **Site3D-user.ini** to **Site3D-enterprise.ini**

To utilise the enterprise configuration, each user need only set the one global **EnterpriseOptionsPath** setting, like this:

* Start Site3D (without opening a project).
* Select the Tools->Options menu option. This will display the Options window. Make sure the **Global** option is selected on the Project drop-down.
* Scrolldown to the **EnterpriseOptionsPath** setting and set it to the shared folder location (from the above example, this would be "P:\Share\Site3D").* Close Site3D. (The settings are only saved when you close Site3D).

#### Updating the Enterprise settings

If you subsequently want to update the enterprise configuration, you would need to first copy the **Site3D-enterprise.ini** file back to the **Site3D-user.ini** in the **%appdata%\Site3D** folder for the user. This must be done when Site3D is not open.
Then simply do the above procedure again, updating the settings as desired, and copying back to the **Site3D-enterprise.ini** file in the shared folder.

### Configuration

On the left of the window is a list containing all the settings that can be altered.

Above the list is a search box that you can type in to quickly find a setting.
As you type, a setting which contains the text will highlight.

Pressing the **Find** button will jump to the next setting which contains the text in the search box.
Holding down the Shift key when you press the **Find** button will search in the reverse direction.

Selecting an item from this list will display a description of the selected setting, shown in a box to the right. Under the description the current value is displayed, this can be set as appropriate for your usage.

Here is the complete list of the currently configurable settings:

| Setting | Description | Data Type/Range | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 3DViewBackGroundColour | Background colour of the 3D view. | RGB Colour | ```  ```  (128, 128, 255) |
| 3DViewFont | Font to use for 3D view texts. | Font | Arial |
| 3DViewRotateRelativeToSurface | When enabled, rotation from horizontal mouse movement will be inverted when the cursor is below the 3D target point and when looking up from below the surface. This can be visualised as dragging the surface point that was under the mouse cursor. | Enabled / Disabled | Disabled |
| 3DViewSeparateFrame | Open the 3D view in a separate window from the main program. For example, to place it on a second screen. | Enabled / Disabled | Disabled |
| 3DViewShow3DHouses | Whether to show the 3D house models in the 3D view. | Enabled / Disabled | Enabled |
| AllowOnlineUpdateCheck | Every time the application is run, check for updates once after loading. | Enabled / Disabled | Enabled |
| AutoSaveToLocalDrive | Auto-save the project to the local drive rather than the project folder. This is useful if the project folder is on a slow network location. | Enabled / Disabled | Disabled |
| CompanyEmployeeName | Name of user to include in reports. | Text |  |
| CompanyName | Company name to include in reports. | Text |  |
| ConcreteProtectionDepthUnderDesign | Minimum depth under design surface before concrete protection is required above the pipe. Depth is surface level to soffit level. | Depth | 1.2m |
| ConcreteProtectionDepthUnderSurvey | Minimum depth under survey surface before concrete protection is required above the pipe. Depth is surface level to soffit level. | Depth | 0.9m |
| ConduitFullPerforatedPermeableAreaRuleset | Ruleset to define permeable area of a fully perforated conduit from a pipe diameter. | Pipe diameter (mm) to permeable area (mm2/m) | ``` <=150=4080, <=225=3120, <=300=3840, <=375=6266, <=450=6327, <=500=6687, <=600=6747, >600=6747 ``` |
| ConduitHalfPerforatedPermeableAreaRuleset | Ruleset to define permeable area of a half perforated conduit from a pipe diameter. | Pipe diameter (mm) to permeable area (mm2/m) | ``` <=150=3060, <=225=2340, <=300=2880, <=375=3133, <=450=3164, <=500=3344, <=600=3374, >600=3374 ``` |
| ConduitAnnotationHeight | Text height for conduit annotations in the plan view. | Height | 0.75m |
| ConstructionDepthBuildingPad | Construction depth under building pads. | Depth | 0.450m |
| ConstructionDepthGarage | Construction depth under garage pads. | Depth | 0.450m |
| ConstructionDepthEarthworks | Construction depth under earthworks and ponds, including their interfacing slopes and steps. | Depth | 0.0m |
| ConstructionDepthInterfacing | Construction depth under road and footpath interfacing slopes and steps. | Depth | 0.0m |
| ConstructionDepthRBTInnerIsland | Construction depth under roundabout inner islands. | Depth | 0.2m |
| ConstructionLineColour | Colour of construction lines. | RGB Colour | ```  ```  (72, 146, 247) |
| CrossSectionBoxHeight | Height of annotation rows in cross-section views. | Height | 2.5m |
| CrossSectionTextHeight | Text height for text drawn in the cross section view. To use this setting, CrossSectionUseManualTextHeight must be enabled. | Height | 0.5m |
| CrossSectionUseManualTextHeight | Whether to use the manual cross section text height. If disabled the text height will be automatically calculated based on the row height. | Enabled / Disabled | Disabled |
| DecimalPlacesPlanChainage | The number of decimal places to display for plan view chainage. Optionally, also the number of additional decimal places to show if they are not zeros. | 0-15 decimal places and 0-7 additional non-zero | 1,0 |
| DecimalPlacesPlanLevels | The number of decimal places to display for plan view level annotations not covered by the more specific settings options. Optionally, also the number of additional decimal places to show if they are not zeros. | 0-15 decimal places and 0-7 additional non-zero | 3,0 |
| DecimalPlacesBuildingFFLLevels | The number of decimal places to display for finish floor level annotations on buildings. Optionally, also the number of additional decimal places to show if they are not zeros. | 0-15 decimal places and 0-7 additional non-zero | 3,0 |
| DecimalPlacesBuildingExternalLevels | The number of decimal places to display for external level annotations around buildings. Optionally, also the number of additional decimal places to show if they are not zeros. | 0-15 decimal places and 0-7 additional non-zero | 2,1 |
| DecimalPlacesPlotBoundaryLevels | The number of decimal places to display for plot boundary level annotations. Optionally, also the number of additional decimal places to show if they are not zeros. | 0-15 decimal places and 0-7 additional non-zero | 2,1 |
| DefaultBackOfFootpathCrossfall | Default footway crossfall when creating a new road. | Gradient, 1 in: | -40.0 |
| DefaultBackOfFootpathWidth | Default back of footway offset when creating a new road. | Offset from channel | 1.8m |
| DefaultChannelCrossfall | Default channel crossfall when creating a new road. | Gradient, 1 in: | 40.0 |

//<!--
show\_header('Section View Display Properties');
show\_index();
//-->

The longsection properties window allows you to adjust the contents and display settings of the longsection view.

### Opening the Longsection View Display Properties Window

While a longsection view window is active, click on the **Section View Display Properties** button ([Icon: Section View Display Properties button](images/tool-buttons/properties.png)) from the view toolbar on the right edge of the window.

You should see the window below:

[Screenshot: longsection-view-display-properties-dlg.png - Longsection view display properties window](images/longsection-view-display-properties-dlg.png)
  
  

### Display

#### Background Colour

The current background colour is shown and can be modified by clicking on the colour block, a window will pop up allowing you to select one of the pre-set colour options or you can define your own custom colour choice.

#### Font

The current font is shown and can be modified by clicking on the button displaying the current font, a window will pop up allowing you to select a font from the list of fonts that are installed on the system.

### Height of Table Rows

#### Row Height

This specifies the height of the annotation boxes.

#### Text Height

By default the text height is determined by the row height and the **Auto** box will be ticked. If you untick the auto box you can manually specify the text height.

### Chainage and Level Annotation

#### Chainage Interval

This specifies the interval at which annotation values appear in the annotation boxes. The default is every 10 metres.

#### Annotate At Interval Only

When ticked the table annotations only occurs at the specified interval. If un-ticked, *vertical IPs* and the *vertical tangent points* will also be annotated.

Regardless of the setting, annotation will always occur at the *start* and *end* points.

#### Compact Chainage

When ticked the chainage annotation neatly squeezes in as many values as possible in crowded regions. Text may be offset slightly to achieve the best fit, but tick marks indicate the true chainage position.

If un-ticked the chainage annotation will be drawn in a uniform style. However, in this annotation style you cannot fit as many text values in crowded regions.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Compact Chainage On | [Screenshot: longsection-annotate-example1.png - Compact Longsection Annotation](images/longsection-annotate-example1.png) |||  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Compact Chainage Off | [Screenshot: longsection-annotate-example2.png - Uniform Longsection Annotation](images/longsection-annotate-example2.png) ||

The longsection drawing shows the change immediately.

#### Annotate at Proposed Levels

With this option ticked the chainage and levels of ground sections will only be annotated at the at the same points as the centreline/main design line.

When unticked the ground sections will be annotated at every change of surface gradient and breaklines on the surface.

  

### Conduit Annotation

You can enable or disable the annotation of pipe material, pipe bedding, and pipe number on the longsection. If these options are enabled they will be annotated with the rest of the pipe information in the drainage invert level rows.

Below is an example of the conduit annotation with all of these options disabled:

[Screenshot: longsection-drainage-display-ex1.png - Longsection Drainage Annotation Example](images/longsection-drainage-display-ex1.png)

And here is an example with all of these options enabled:

[Screenshot: longsection-drainage-display-ex2.png - Longsection Drainage Annotation Example](images/longsection-drainage-display-ex2.png)

As shown above, displaying the pipe bedding will add an additional line of text and removes a portion of the line which would otherwise overlap the additional text.

#### Annotation Style

Here you can pick either *Centred multi-line text* or *Separate lines of text*.

*Centred multi-line text*: With this option selected, pipe annotations will be drawn as a single vertically centred block of multi-line text. This style is shown in the above conduit annotation example images. When exported to CAD, these annotations will be exported as a single MText entity.

*Separate lines of text*: With this option selected, each line of the pipe annotation text will be drawn (and exported to CAD) as separate text entities with separate vertical alignment settings. Because of this, the top and bottom lines of text are vertically aligned to the edges of the annotation row. Below is an example of this in Site3D:

[Screenshot: longsection-drainage-display-ex3.png - Longsection Drainage Annotation Example](images/longsection-drainage-display-ex3.png)

With the pipe bedding annotation enabled the two annotation styles produce very similar results. Shown below is an example of the separate lines of text style with pipe bedding annotation enabled:

[Screenshot: longsection-drainage-display-ex4.png - Longsection Drainage Annotation Example](images/longsection-drainage-display-ex4.png)  

### Y Axis Display

Here you can set the Y axis annotation and grid line settings.

If **Annotate Along Axis** is ticked you will get level annotations along the axis at the specified interval, with level annotations every major interval.

If **Draw Grid Lines** is ticked then you will get horizontal lines drawn across the long section at the specified interval, with major interval lines highlighted.

There are text-boxes that allow you to specify the Minor Interval and how many minor intervals comprises a major interval.

The minor and major tick interval colours are shown and can be modified by clicking on the colour blocks. Clicking on one of the colour blocks will open a colour picker window where you can select a new colour.

  

### Alignment Annotation

Here you can specify how the horizontal and vertical alignment gets annotated.

#### Text Only:

No special annotation.

[Screenshot: longsection-alignment-annote-ex1.png - Longsection horizontal/vertical alignment annotation](images/longsection-alignment-annote-ex1.png)

#### Display Curves:

Vertical Alignment: Curves will be drawn to indicate either a hog or sag curve in the vertical alignment instead of text specifying the curve type.

Horizontal Alignment: Curves will be drawn to indicate the direction of a turn in the horizontal alignment instead of text specifying that there is a curve.

[Screenshot: longsection-alignment-annote-ex2.png - Longsection horizontal/vertical alignment annotation with curves](images/longsection-alignment-annote-ex2.png)

#### Display Lines:

Vertical Alignment: Lines will be drawn to indicate the gradient direction instead of text specifying that it is a gradient.

Horizontal Alignment: Lines will be drawn to visually indicate each straight instead of text specifying that it is a straight.

[Screenshot: longsection-alignment-annote-ex3.png - Longsection horizontal/vertical alignment annotation with lines](images/longsection-alignment-annote-ex3.png)

#### Curves & Lines:

Both curves and lines will be visually annotated as noted for the individual options above.

[Screenshot: longsection-alignment-annote-ex4.png - Longsection horizontal/vertical alignment annotation with curves and lines](images/longsection-alignment-annote-ex4.png)
  

### Raised Table Levels

This option allows you to choose how the centreline and channel levels are annotated when the road has a speed table.

[Screenshot: longsection-raised-table-levels.png - Raised Platform annotation](images/longsection-raised-table-levels.png)

**Levels of centreline under raised table**: specifies that the annotated centreline/channel levels will match the underlying centreline/channel lines that are below any raised platforms. The longsection will automatically include an annotation on the section specifying that the raised platform levels are above the quoted centreline levels (shown in the image above). This option is useful when the raised platform is
short and is more of an addition to the road construction.

**Levels of raised table surface**: specifies that the annotated centreline/channel levels match the top of the designed surface, be that road or raised speed table. This option is useful
when the raised platform is longer, either partial or entire road length, and is designed as a shared surface.

  

### Gradient Limits

Here you can specify the minimum and maximum gradients that you want in your design.

If a gradient is outside of this design specification then it will be highlighted to you in red in the longsection annotation.

[Screenshot: longsection-gradient-outside-range-ex.png - Example of a gradient highlighted due to it being outside of the specified limits](images/longsection-gradient-outside-range-ex.png)
  

### Row Ordering

On the left of the window is a list of the longsection row labels.
The labels appear in the order the corresponding rows are drawn in the longsection view, from top to bottom.

Rows can be quickly reordered using drag and drop functionality. This is done by clicking on an item, keeping the mouse button held down and dragging the row the desired position, then releasing the mouse button to drop the row to the new position.

You can also move rows up or down one row at a time, which will swap the row with the row above or below respectively. This is done by clicking on one of the rows and then clicking on one of buttons below the row list to move it up or down. Alternatively, you can right click on a row and select **Move Up** or **Move Down** from the right click menu.

The row titled "- Section Drawing -" indicates the position of the section drawing.
Rows that come before this item in the row list will be drawn above the sections, while rows after this are drawn below the sections.

### Label Editing

Row labels can be edited by clicking on a row and then clicking the **Edit Text** button below the row list, or by right clicking on a row and selecting Edit Label from the right click menu.

The selected item will change to a textbox that you can type into:

[Screenshot: longsection-row-label-edit-ex.png - Editing a label in the list](images/longsection-row-label-edit-ex.png)

The following rows cannot be edited: Section Drawing, Ground Section Level Rows, Substratum Level Rows.
This is because these are placeholder rows for additional content to be inserted and as such do not have their own labels.
For example, the "Substratum Level Rows" controls the position that substratum rows are added, but there may no substratum rows or multiple substratum rows, and the individual substratum rows will use the corresponding substratum name as the row label.

* [Long-section properties](longsection-properties.htm)

In order to save or print results you must have a valid License for the software.

To see the License Window click the **Help** menu and select the **License...** option.

[Screenshot: help-license-menu.png - License option on the Help menu](images/help-license-menu.png)

*Note*: The License Window will also be shown if a save or print operation is attempted when no license is present.

[Screenshot: license-dlg-ex1.png - License Window](images/license-dlg-ex1.png)

To obtain a License to activate the save and print functions you should contact your reseller. The big button in the centre of the window will usually take you to your reseller's website.

### Standalone, Network or Cloud License

A *standalone* license is specific to a particular computer and can activate the software only on that one computer.

A *network* license can activate the software on any computer on your local or wide area network. It just requires our network license server application to be installed on one of your computers.

A *cloud* license can activate the software on any computer that is connected to the internet. It just requires knowledge of your cloud license log-in details.

If you have a standalone license, follow the guide on this page. If you have a network or cloud license, follow the guide on [the network or cloud license help page](network-license.htm).

### Serial Number

When obtaining a License you will need to give the Serial Number which is shown on this License Window. (You can highlight and *Copy* the Serial Number from the window so that you can *Paste* it into a message).

The Serial Number is unique for each computer, and the corresponding license activation code will only work for the particular machine.

*Note*: The serial number is not editable.

### User Name

When obtaining a License you also need to specify a User Name. You can choose the User Name, but it should normally be the name of your organisation or your personal name. Your reseller may assign the User Name to you.

### License

When you receive the license activation code (usually supplied by email) enter it into the **License** box on this window.
As soon as you have entered a valid license code the status message will update accordingly:

[Screenshot: license-dlg-ex2.png - Valid license status example](images/license-dlg-ex2.png)

Click the **OK** button. The software will remember your license details and (if the license is valid) the save and print functions will be active.

If you have entered the License code correctly and the status is still not shown as valid, then check the following:

* Make sure the User Name is entered correctly. The User Name will be supplied along with the License code. Both the License code and the User Name must be correct for the license to validate.
* Make sure the Serial Number shown on the license window matches the one that accompanies the supplied License code. If it doesn't check the following:
  + Are you using the same computer for which you requested the license? Each License activation code is specific to the particular computer. (If you need a floating network license contact your reseller for options)
  + Has the hardware profile of the computer significantly changed since you requested the License? For example, changing the motherboard will normally cause the Serial Number to change. In this case you will need to contact your reseller for a re-activation code.
  + Have you connected additional hardware since requesting the License? Occasionally, connecting USB devices or WiFi devices can cause a change to the Serial Number. Try to put the machine back into the state it was in when you requested the License. You can then enter the License activation code when the Serial Number matches. Once the License is validated you should be able to reconnect your extra devices without affecting the License.

### Time Limited License

Time limited licenses may be supplied upon request. Contact your reseller to request a time limited license.
A time limited License will permit full functionality of the software (including saving and printing) for a limited number of days.

If you have been supplied with a time limited license, enter the User Name and License code as described above for a permanent license.

When the time limited license is recognised, the status on the License window will indicate how many days remain in the temporary period:

[Screenshot: license-dlg-ex3.png - Valid license status example](images/license-dlg-ex3.png)

*Note*: To maximise your license period, you should enter your time limited license as soon as you get it because it will expire on a pre-set date.

---

In order to save or print results you must have a valid License for the software.

Click here for information on [installing and setting up a network license server](network-server-install.htm).

To see the License Window click the **Help** menu and select the **License...** option.

[Screenshot: help-license-menu.png - License option on the Help menu](images/help-license-menu.png)

*Note*: The License Window will also be shown if a save or print operation is attempted when no license is present.

[Screenshot: license-dlg-ex4.png - License Window](images/license-dlg-ex4.png)

To obtain a License to activate the save and print functions you should contact your reseller. The big button in the centre of the window will usually take you to your reseller's website.

### Network, Cloud or Standalone License

A *standalone* license is specific to a particular computer and can activate the software only on that one computer.

A *network* license can activate the software on any computer on your local or wide area network. It just requires our network license server application to be installed on one of your computers.

A *cloud* license can activate the software on any computer that is connected to the internet. It just requires knowledge of your cloud license log-in details.

If you have a network or cloud license, follow the guide on this page. If you have a standalone license, follow the guide on [the standalone license help page](license.htm).

### Network : License Server

For a **network license**, enter the name of your local computer on which the [Microplot Network License Server](network-server-install.htm) has been set-up.
Then click the **Check** button.

*Note*: If you have previously entered the server name, it will already be shown here, with the license status indicated in the **License** item.

[Screenshot: license-dlg-ex5.png - Valid license status example](images/license-dlg-ex5.png)

### Check

If you have entered the server name, and a network license was not attained, the **Check** button will remain active so that you can check the server again.
Failure to attain a network license could be due to any of the following:

* The License Server computer name is entered incorrectly.
* The Microplot Network License Server is not set up on the server computer.
* The Microplot Network License Server is not configured with licenses for this software product.

### Cloud : Username and Password

For a **cloud license**, enter the username and password combination that constitute your cloud log-in details. Then click the **Check** button.

*Note*: As the password is hidden from view, it may be easiest to copy+paste it.

[Screenshot: license-dlg-ex6.png - Valid license status example](images/license-dlg-ex6.png)

### Check

If you have entered the username and password, and a cloud license was not attained, the **Check** button will remain active so that you can check the cloud again.
Failure to attain a cloud license could be due to any of the following:

* Your computer may not be connected to the internet.
* The username or password may be mis-typed. Try copy+pasting instead of manually typing.
* All your cloud licenses are already in use by your other users. I this case the Status button will be shown so that you can assess who is using them.

### Status

If the license server is serving licenses for this product, then the **Status** button will be active.
You can click the Status button to see further information about the License Server and the current license usage.

[Screenshot: license-status-dlg.png - License server status](images/license-status-dlg.png)

You can scroll down the status window to see which local users are currently utilising the network licenses, and for how long they have been using them.

[Screenshot: license-status-dlg2.png - License server status](images/license-status-dlg2.png)

This information can be useful when all available licenses are currently in use. You may be able to ask another user to close the software on their computer so that you can utilise it on your computer.

### Firewalls and Security

Site3D needs to be able to communicate with the license server via the network (for network licenses) and the Internet (for cloud licenses). In most cases, there will be no issue with security software or firewalls. In same cases, if you have third party security software installed or a network firewall, depending on its configuration, it may initially block this communication. If this is the case, the Site3D license window may give no response for about 20 seconds while it tries unsuccessfully to establish the communication.

If you do experience an issue with the network or cloud license, you may need to configure your security system to permit Site3D to make outgoing communication on TCP port 443 (cloud license) or 7017 (network license).

The Microplot Network License Server allows you to use your product's LAN or WAN licenses on any of the computers on your network. If you currently use standalone licenses, which are locked to a specific computer, you may like to consider upgrading to network licenses. Please contact your reseller for advice.

A LAN License can be used by any end-user on your local area network (at one office building). A WAN License can be used by any end-user on your wide area network (between multiple office locations, or for home and office usage).

### Choose a Computer to be the Server

When installing the Microplot Network License Server you should pick a computer on your network that will be running all the time. This computer will then be the *License Server*. The License server computer will need to be running whenever you want to use the product licenses on any of your other computers.

The License Server computer can be any machine running Microsoft Windows Vista, Windows 7, Windows 8, Windows 10, Windows 11 or Windows Server.
This can be either 32 bit Windows or 64 bit Windows.

*Note*: You must be logged on as a user with Administrator rights on the computer in order to install the Microplot Network License Server.

Follow this step-by-step guide to install the Microplot Network License Server:

### Install the Network License Server

Run the set-up program as **administrator**. To do this:

* Save the installer file to a folder on your hard-drive.
* Use *Windows Explorer* to browse to that folder.
* **Right-click** the mouse over the installer file and select **Run as administrator** from the menu. (See example image below)

[Screenshot: mnls-install-ex1.png - Install Network License Server](images/mnls-install-ex1.png)

You may be prompted for confirmation to run the installer. Click **Yes**:

[Screenshot: mnls-install-ex2.png - Install Network License Server](images/mnls-install-ex2.png)

Click **Next** on the installer welcome window:

[Screenshot: mnls-install-ex3.png - Install Network License Server](images/mnls-install-ex3.png)

Read and accept the license agreement, and click **Next**:

Accept the default install folder, or browse to your preferred folder:

[Screenshot: mnls-install-ex5.png - Install Network License Server](images/mnls-install-ex5.png)

*Important*: Please make a note of the install folder location, because this is where you'll need to put the license configuration file in order to activate the licenses.

*Note*: If you do not get the folder selection option then you already have the License Server installed. The installer will upgrade it. If you need to change the install folder, then first uninstall the License server (*Start->Control Panel->Programs & Features*) before you run the installer.

Click the **Install** button:

[Screenshot: mnls-install-ex6.png - Install Network License Server](images/mnls-install-ex6.png)

Finally, click the **Finish** button:

[Screenshot: mnls-install-ex7.png - Install Network License Server](images/mnls-install-ex7.png)

### Verify the Server is Running

There are a few ways you can verify the network license server is now running:

* In the license window on Site3D, select the *Network* option, enter the name of the License Server and click the *Check* button. If the license server is reachable, then the *Check* button will change to a *Status...* button. pressing the *Status...* button will show the server status, including its *serial number*.
* Or, use Windows Explorer to look in the install folder. You should see a file called `LicenseServerLog.log`. You can view the contents of the log file in any text viewer like Notepad. The entries in the log file should confirm that the license server is running, and show the *serial number* for the server.
* Or, in the Windows Task Manager, select the *Services* tab, and look for the name *MicroplotLicenseServer* and verify that the status is *Running*.

### Firewalls and Security

Microplot Network License Server needs to be able to receive connections from computers on your local network, and accordingly, the installer creates an appropriate access rule for the Windows Firewall. If you are using no other security products then you're ready to continue.

However, if you are using another security product (for example, a third-party firewall or anti-virus system) then you may need to configure it.
You only need to do this if you've verified in the Windows Task Manager that the Microplot Network License Server is running, but you can't connect to it using Site3D from another local computer. In this case, you may need to refer to the documentation which came with your security product to permit incoming connections on port 7017 to the Microplot Network License Server.

### Server Registration

In order to activate network licenses for Site3D, you will need to register your Network License Server and obtain a network license configuration file.

To do this you need to provide your reseller with the *serial number* generated by the Microplot Network License Server.
You can see the serial number in one of two ways:

* In the license window on Site3D, select the *Network* option, enter the name of the License Server and click the *Check* button. If the license server is reachable, then the *Check* button will change to a *Status...* button. Pressing the *Status...* button will show the server status, including the *serial number*.
* Use Windows Explorer to look in the install folder. You should see a file called `LicenseServerLog.log`. You can view the contents of the log file in any text viewer like Notepad. The entries in the log file should confirm that the license server is running, and show the *serial number* for the server.

### License Configuration File

After you have registered your license server, your reseller can provide you with a license configuration file to activate your purchased network licenses.

Simply save the license configuration file, `config.lic`, into the install folder.

*Note*: The default install folder is `C:\Program Files\Microplot Network License Server` on a 32 bit Windows computer, and `C:\Program Files (x86)\Microplot Network License Server` on a 64 bit Windows computer.

The running Microplot Network License Server will automatically detect the file, and start serving the licenses for usage on your local network. *Note*: It may take up to 15 minutes for the new licenses to become active.

You should now be able to utilise the network licenses with Site3D running on computers on your local network. See the [help page on Network Licenses](network-license.htm) for further information.

### Replacement License Configuration Files

When you renew your support and maintenance for Site3D each year, you may be provided with a replacement `config.lic` file for that new support period.

To apply the new `config.lic` file, simply save it into the install folder to replace the previous one.

The running Microplot Network License Server will automatically detect the new file, and start serving the licenses for usage on your local network. *Note*: It may take up to 15 minutes for the new licenses to become active.

https://www.site3d.co.uk/help/glossary.h:
Site3D - Road and Site Design

//<!--
show\_header('Glossary of Terms');
show\_index();
//-->

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Centreline | The line, normally down the centre of a road carriageway, from which all the road geometry is derived. |
| Chainage | The horizontal distance along a route. For example, the centreline chainage is the distance along the centreline following the curves of the road. The chainage is the 2D plan distance which does not take into account variations in the vertical. The chainage only represents the true distance when the route is completely horizontal. Where the route is not horizontal the true distance is longer than the chainage distance. |
| Channel | The channel is edge of the road carriageway at the base of the kerb. The channel is normally where rainwater would collect and run along to a gulley. Where the road carriageway slopes down away from the kerb (for example, with super-elevation round a curve) the edge line is still referred to as the channel. |
| Driveway | The vehicular access to a property. Sometimes known as a *vehicle crossing*. Comprised of a kerb ramp and a footway crossing |
| Gulley | A gulley is the drainage point on a channel where rainwater drains off the carriageway. |
| Fillet | A fillet is a curve which tangentially fits into an angle to round it off. For example, a road centreline normally comprises straight lengths joined by fillet curves so that it is a smooth path. |
| Footpath | A pedestrian walkway. It is common for the term to denote a *footway*, though a footpath can generally be any walkway not adjacent to a road. |
| Footway | The pedestrian path at the side of a road. |
| ICD | Inscribed Circle Diameter. This refers to diameter of the outermost circle of the roundabout. The term ICD is often used to refer to the circle itself rather than the diameter. |
||  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Interface | An interface is where the edge of the new designed surface joins with the existing ground. Commonly the designed level is interfaced to the ground level by means of a slope of given grade. |
| Invert | The lowest point on the interior circumference of a pipe, or the bottom of a manhole |
| Kerb | The step up at the edge of the road carriageway, often onto the footway. The kerb face usually makes a channel to collect water at the edge of the road. |
| Pram Crossing | A cut down kerb which improves accessibility for baby carriages, wheeled or mobility impaired pedestrians. |
| Soffit | The highest point on the interior circumference of a pipe. Opposite of *invert* |
| Station | The horizontal distance along a route. For example, the centreline station is the distance along the centreline following the curves of the road. The station is the 2D plan distance which does not take into account variations in the vertical. The station only represents the true distance when the route is completely horizontal. Where the route is not horizontal the true distance is longer than the station distance. |
| Transition | A curve smoothly varying from one radius to another. Geometrically, the transition curve is part of a spiral. A transition would normally be used on a highway either side of a fixed radius bend, to smoothly change the driving path from the straight (infinite radius) to the fixed radius. If a transition was not present the driver would be required to turn the steering wheel instantaneously at the start and end of the curve. |
|  |  |

From time to time, Site3D checks on-line for new versions becoming available.

When a new version has been released, a notification is shown at the bottom right corner of the screen.

*Note*: This facility requires an internet connection.

### Automatic Update Notification

If you are covered by a current [support and maintenance](support-key.htm) contract, then the notification will offer to download and update the software:

[Screenshot: update-notify-dlg.png](images/update-notify-dlg.png)

Press the **Download** button to action the download and update.

The download progress is shown:

[Screenshot: download-dlg.png](images/download-dlg.png)

And when downloaded, it will offer to run the installer to perform the update:

[Screenshot: update-ready-dlg.png](images/update-ready-dlg.png)

*Note*: Clicking **Close and Update** will close the program immediately. If you have an open project with unsaved changes that you want to keep, then click **Cancel** instead and save your work. You can restart the update process from the **Help->Check for updates...** menu option.

[Screenshot: update-wizard.png](images/update-wizard.png)

Click the **Next** button, read and accept the End User License Agreement, and click the **Install** button.

When the update has completed, it will offer to restart Site3D for you.

### Manual Check for Updates

Use the **Help->Check for updates...** menu option at any time for check for the latest version of the software.
The following window is shown:

*Note*: This facility requires an internet connection.

[Screenshot: check-for-updates-dlg.png](images/check-for-updates-dlg.png)

*NOTE*: If the **Yes, automatically check for updates** option is selected, the software will automatically notify you of new versions.

Click the **Check online now** button to determine the latest version of the software.
If a newer version is available, then the following window is shown:

[Screenshot: download-dlg2.png](images/download-dlg2.png)

Click the **Yes, download and install** button to proceed with the upgrade. Or, the **Options..** button will take you back to the **Update Check Settings** window.

The file menu contains options related to opening or saving files and data.

[Screenshot: file-menu.png - The File Menu](images/file-menu.png)

### Open

This menu option lets you open a project from any of the compatible file types. See the [help page for opening a drawing](file-open.htm) for more information and file types that are compatible with Site3D.

### Close

Clicking on this menu option will close the current project and all windows related to the project.

### Save

This will save the currently open project. If the project has not been previously saved then this will open the "Save As..." window.

### Save As

The Save As menu option has a list of all the file types you can save to or export to. See the [File Output help page](file-save-as.htm) for more information.

### Import

When you have opened a project using the File Open functionality you can then import further files to incorporate into the project. See the [File Import help page](file-import.htm) for more information.

### Push to AutoCAD

You can export data from Site3D directly to a running instance of AutoCAD using this menu option. See the [Push to AutoCAD help page](push-to-autocad.htm) for more information.

### Pull from AutoCAD

You can import data directly from a running instance of AutoCAD into Site3D using this menu option. See the [Push to AutoCAD help page](push-to-autocad.htm) for more information.

### Print/Plot

This allows you to print out a drawing. See the [Print/Plot help page](plot.htm) for more information.

### Most Recently Used Files

In this section you will see a numbered list of files that have most recently been opened with Site3D.

### License Site3D

Clicking this menu option will bring up the Site3D licensing window. See the [Licensing help page](license.htm) for more information.

### Exit

This will close all the open projects and exit Site3D. Before closing you will be asked if you want to save any projects with unsaved data, once all projects have been safely closed down Site3D will exit.

---

#### See Also:

* [Contents](index.htm)
* [User Interface Introduction](user-interface.htm)
Site3D - Road and Site Design

//<!--
show\_header('Edit Menu');
show\_index();
//-->

The Edit Menu contains useful functionality for copying data to or from Site3D and finding elements within Site3D.

[Screenshot: edit-menu.png - The Edit Menu](images/edit-menu.png)

### Undo

This allows you to undo an action within Site3D, and shows which action will be undone.

You can also execute this action using the keyboard shortcut **Control + Z**.

For more information see the [Undo help page](undo.htm).

### Copy

This option will pop up a window which will allow you to select elements from Site3D to copy to the clipboard, allowing you to later paste this into any CAD system that is compatible with the AutoCAD clipboard format.

You can also execute this action using the keyboard shortcut **Control + C**.

When this action is performed you will see the following window pop up:

[Screenshot: copy-to-clipboard.png - Copy to clipboard preview window](images/copy-to-clipboard.png)

This window allows you to select the information that you want to copy.

Beside some minor cosmetic differences the copy to clipboard tool is functionally the same as the [Push to AutoCAD](push-to-autocad.htm) tool.
For more information about the various options when copying see the [Push to AutoCAD help page](push-to-autocad.htm).

### Paste

This option will paste CAD elements from the clipboard into Site3D, allowing you to transfer design elements that have been copied from your CAD software.

You can also execute this action using the keyboard shortcut **Control + V**.

### Find

This option will bring up a prompt allowing you to search for the given text within the names of design elements on your site.

You can also execute this action using the keyboard shortcut **Control + F**.

[Screenshot: find.png - Find window](images/find.png)

### Find Next

If you have used the Find option to search for a design element on your site this will automatically jump to the next design item that matches the search criteria.

You can also execute this action using the keyboard shortcut **Shift + F3**.

### Screenshot

This command will capture a high quality screenshot of the current view into your clipboard. You can then paste this into the editing software of your choosing.

The view menu contains useful functionality for modifying the views or changing what content is shown in a view.

[Screenshot: view-menu-0.png - View Menu part 1](images/view-menu-0.png)

### Toolbars

Here you will find a list of design toolbars that you can activate in the current view, allowing you to activate a specific toolbar from the menu.

For an overview of the various design toolbars available in Site3D see [The Design Toolbar help page](design-toolbar.htm).

---

### Zoom

[Screenshot: view-zoom-menu.png - Zoom in](images/view-zoom-menu.png)

The functionality of many of the zoom tools available in this menu are also available in the [View Toolbar](view-tools.htm).

#### Zoom In

This menu option will will zoom the view in by a small amount.

#### Zoom Out

This menu option will zoom the view out by a small amount.

#### Zoom Extents

This menu option will activate zoom the view so that the site extents are visible. By default the site extents match the drawing extents, so the default behaviour will be the same as Zoom All. However, you can set the site extents from the [Window Menu](window-menu.htm), using the Set Extent to Current View menu option.

#### Zoom All

This menu option will activate zoom the view so that the entire drawing is visible.

#### Zoom Realtime

This tool will allow you to zoom the current view precisely based on the vertical movement of the mouse.

[Icon: Realtime zoom cursor](images/zoom-realtime.png)
When selected you will see the cursor icon change to the realtime zoom cursor. Click and hold the left mouse button and drag the mouse up or down to interactively zoom the current view in or out.

#### Zoom Window

This menu option will activate the Zoom to Region tool, which will zoom the view so that the selected region fills the view window. This tool can also be accessed from the [View Toolbar](view-tools.htm).

---

### Separate Views

[Screenshot: separate-views.png - Zoom in](images/separate-views.png)

Under the Separate Views option in the view menu you will find the following options in a further menu: **Separate 3D View**, **Separate Long Section View**, and **Separate Cross Section View**. These allow you to open a view externally to the main Site3D window, the separate views will act like independent windows within the operating system. Separating a view will allow you to use the operating systems ability to dock and tile windows, for example this will allow you to have Site3D full screen on your primary monitor and have another view full screen on another monitor.

---

### Pan Realtime

This option is only available in plan view, when selected this will activate the Pan tool, allowing you to grab and move the view using the mouse cursor. This tool can also be accessed from the [View Toolbar](view-tools.htm).

### Previous/Next View

This menu option will modify the view to match a previous view after a zoom command was used.

---

### Find Site

The *Find Site* facility is very useful when you load a drawing which appears blank on the screen.
This is usually due to the drawing's extents being huge in relation to the area of interest. The drawing appears to be blank because the useful drawing is so tiny in relation to the extents that it is hidden within a single pixel on the screen.

The *Find Site* facility will examine the imported drawing entities to work out the likely area of interest and will zoom the display to show it.

*Note*: Once you have found the site with this option, it is generally useful to then use the [Surface->Delete/Crop Region...](surface-tools.htm) facility to reset the drawing extents to the useful site.

---

[Screenshot: view-menu-1.png - View Menu part 2](images/view-menu-1.png)

### Layers

This menu option will open the project layers window. See the [Layers Window help page](layers-dlg.htm) for more information.

### Project Statistics

This menu option will open the project statistics window, shown below.

[Screenshot: project-statistics.png - Project Statistics Window](images/project-statistics.png)

The Project Statistic window shows you the number of different entities that appear throughout the site.

At the top is a list of models which breaks down into the entity types and specifies the number and additional details for each entity.

At the bottom is a combined total for each entity next to the visual representation of the entity. Hovering over the images will show a tool tip which specifies which entity the image corresponds to.

---

### Display Menu

This sections toggles on/off different view settings.

[Screenshot: view-menu-2.png - View Menu part 3](images/view-menu-2.png)

**Show Surface Colouring:** This option toggles the display of the survey surface in plan view. By default the surface is coloured with high points drawing in orange and low points drawing in blue. You can modify how the surface is coloured using the [Model Colour Properties](model-colours.htm).

**Show 3D Controls:** This option is only available whilst the 3D view is active and toggles the 3D control panel window, providing a set of controls to alter the 3D view. For more information see the [3D Controls Window help page](3d-controls.htm).

**Show Flow Arrows:** This option toggles the display of flow arrows on the current survey, the arrows colour indicates the gradient with arrow pointing in the gradient fall direction.

**Flow arrow gradient to colour key**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | 1:10 or steeper |
|  | 1:10 -> 1:20 |
|  | 1:20 -> 1:50 |
|  | 1:50 -> 1:80 |
|  | 1:80 -> 1:100 |
|  | 1:100 -> 1:130 |
| [Screenshot: black\_white\_box.png - Black or White Colour](images/black\_white\_box.png) | flatter than 1:130 |

**Show Triangles Wire Frame:** This option toggles the display of the triangle wire frame of the survey triangulation.

**Show Road Directions:** This option toggles the display of arrowheads along centrelines which indicate the road direction, pointing from low chainage to high chainage.

**Show Channel Items:** This option toggles the display of channel items. Coloured blocks will be drawn along the centreline to indicate different channel items.

**Show Vertical Channel Items:** This option toggles the display of vertical channel items. Lines will be drawn at intervals along the centreline to the channels with arrows pointing toward the channel for a transition to a lower crossfall, and arrows pointing from the channel to the centreline indicating a transition to a greater crossfall.

**Show Vertical Kerb Items:** This option toggles the display of vertical kerb items. Red lines will be drawn either side of the channel along the section where there is a vertical kerb transition.

**Show Curve Tangents:** This option is only available in plan view and toggles the display of curve tangents. Gray lines will be drawn showing the two tangent lines that intersect to the curve IP point.

**Show Gouraud Shading:** This option is only available whilst the 3D view is active and toggles the shading method of the triangulation. Gouraud shading is a lighting method which interpolates across the surface of the triangles, giving a smoother appearance to the triangulation. The issue with this method is that the triangles blend together and the surface can become less distinct.

**Show Import DXF Details:** This option toggles the display of DXF importing information. A window will appear showing a log of information when you are importing a DXF file.

**Show Outfalls:** This option is on by default, selecting this option toggles the highlighting of outfalls in the drainage networks. Outfalls will be highlighted with a green circle.

**Show Drainage Runs:** This options toggles the highlighting of runs in the drainage networks. Drainage will be coloured according to the run number, allowing you to see the drainage runs of a network at a glance.

**Show Manholes included in Schedule:** This option is only available in plan view and toggles the highlighting of manholes which have the "Included in schedules" flag ticked in the [Manhole Properties](manhole-properties.htm). A yellow triangle will be drawn behind manholes. Note this option will turn off "Show Manholes with Manual Cover Levels".

**Show Manholes with Manual Cover Levels:** This option is only available in plan view and toggles the highlighting of manholes which have the "Manual" flag ticked in the [Manhole Properties](manhole-properties.htm). A yellow triangle will be drawn behind manholes. Note this option will turn off "Show Manholes included in Schedule".

**Show Rulers:** This option is only available in plan view and toggles the drawing of rulers at the edges of the view. The rulers annotate the coordinates for the x and y axis.

---

[Screenshot: view-menu-3.png - View Menu part 4](images/view-menu-3.png)  
[Screenshot: view-menu-4.png - View Menu part 5](images/view-menu-4.png)  
[Screenshot: view-menu-5.png - View Menu part 6](images/view-menu-5.png)

### Display 3D View

This option will open the 3D view window allowing you to view your site in 3D. You can also open the 3D view by using the [3D view button](model-view-toolbar.htm#3d-view-button) in the [model view toolbar](model-view-toolbar.htm).

### View Location in Google Earth

This option will open Google Earth if installed and set the view to the site location.

### Drive Through

This option is only available whilst the 3D view is active and will bring up the drive through window. You can select which road you want to move along, set the height from the road surface you want the view to be, and the speed to move along the road. Below that is a slider which indicates the position along the road, clicking the play button will start moving the camera along the centreline.
*Note:* This is not simulated vehicle tracking and is just another way of viewing your site in 3D.

### Set 3D View Camera/Target

This option is only available whilst the 3D view is active and will allow you to place points in the plan view to set the camera position and target position. The camera position will be the place that you are viewing from in the 3D view and will be indicated as a green cross in the plan view. The target position will be the point of interest that you are looking at in the 3D view and will be indicated as a red cross in the plan view. The crosses that get drawn in the plan view will disappear once the plan view is moved or zoomed.

### Properties

This option will open the view properties for the current view.
If you are in the plan view you will see the [Project Options](project-options.htm) window appear.
In the 3D view you will see the 3D view properties window appear.
In a longsection view you will see the [Section View Display Properties](longsection-display-settings.htm) window appear.
In a cross section view you will see the [Cross Section Properties](centreline-cross-sections.htm) window appear.

---

* [User Interface Introduction](user-interface.htm)

//<!--
show\_header('Surface Menu');

The Surface menu contains options related to surveys.

[Screenshot: surface-menu.png - The Surface Menu](images/surface-menu.png)

### 2D to 3D convert

This menu option opens the [2D to 3D survey converter](2d-3d.htm) window. See the [Convert 2D to 3D help page](2d-3d.htm) for more information.

### Delete/Crop Region

Selecting this menu option will start the delete region tool from the surface tools. See the [Delete Region section of the Surface Tools help page](surface-tools.htm#delete_region) for more information.

### Drape To Surface

Drape to surface will open a window allowing you to select a model to drape to the current survey surface. This will keep the horizontal information so that the 2D/plan view remains the same while matching the vertical information to the survey surface.

### Edit Surface

Clicking on this option will open the [Surface Tools](surface-tools.htm) toolbar.

### Explode Blocks

In CAD it is possible to combine multiple drawing items into a single "block", allowing for easy duplication/reuse.
the Explode Blocks option will separate these blocks into the individual components that they are composed from.

### Add/Edit Section

Clicking on this option will open the [Simple Section](simple-section.htm) toolbar.

### Contour

This menu option will open the [Survey Contours](automatic-surface-contouring.htm) window, this can also be accessed by pressing the Contour Surface button [Icon: Survey Contours button](images/survey-contours-button.png) on the [Model View Toolbar](model-view-toolbar.htm).

### Volumes

This menu option will open the [Surface Volume](surface-volume.htm) window where you can get the calculated cut and fill volumes from a proposed surface and an existing surface. You can also save the generated volume model for viewing as a cut and fill banded isopachyte surface. See the [Surface Volume help page](surface-volume.htm) for more information.

### Formation Surface Properties

This menu option will open the [Formation Surface Properties](automatic-formation-surface.htm) window where you can toggle which items are included in the formation surface and the construction depth settings used when generating the formation surface.

### Create Formation Surface

Clicking on this option will generate the formation surface model and add it to your project. See the [Automated Site Formation Surface help page](automatic-formation-surface.htm) for more information.

### Create Final Surface

This will generate the final surface model and add it to your project. See the [Final Surface help page](final-surface.htm) for more information.

### Triangulation

This menu item has two options in a sub menu Force Re-Triangulation, and Show Triangles Wire Frame. Force Re-Triangulation will force the current survey to be triangulated again from the points that make up the surface. Show Triangles Wire Frame will draw the lines that form each triangle edge.

### Water flow from point

Clicking this menu option will allow you to select a surface see the water flow from the mouse position. A line is drawn from the mouse cursor to the local lowest point, following the direction of the steepest downhill gradient. The flow path updates in realtime as the mouse position changes.

### Scale

This menu option will allow you to scale a model in the project. See [Model Scale](model-scale.htm) for more information.

### Translate

This menu option will allow you to move a model in the project. See [Model Translate](model-translate.htm) for more information.

### Rotate

This menu option will allow you to rotate a model in the project. See [Model Rotate](model-rotate.htm) for more information.

### GeoReference

The GeoReference menu item will allow you to apply GeoReferencing to a model or image in the project. See [Model GeoRereference help page](model-georeference.htm) for more information.

### Borehole Tools

Clicking on this option will open the [Borehole Tools](borehole-tools.htm) toolbar.

//<!--
show\_header('Road Menu');

//-->

The road menu contains options related to centrelines.

[Screenshot: centreline-menu-translate.png - The Road Menu](images/centreline-menu-translate.png)

### Add/Edit Centreline

This menu option opens the [Road Tools](centreline-tools.htm). See the [Road Tools help page](centreline-tools.htm) for more information.

### Centreline Extras

Under the [Centreline Extras](centreline-extras.htm) sub menu there are options for levelling a centreline to a surface or plane, and an option to set the start chainage of a centreline. See the [Centreline Extras help page](centreline-extras.htm) for more information.

### Cross Sections

This sub menu allows you to attach or detach [Simple Sections](simple-section.htm) to a centreline as cross sections. This is useful for the [Save As Hec-Ras](file-save-as.htm) functionality.

### Channel/Footpaths Tools

Clicking on this menu option will open the [Channel/Footpaths Tools](channel-tools.htm).

### Secondary Channel Tools

The Drainage menu contains options related to drainage networks.

[Screenshot: drainage-menu.png - The Surface Menu](images/drainage-menu.png)

### Add/Edit Drainage Run

This menu option opens the [Drainage Tools](drainage.htm) toolbar.

### Automatic Gully Properties

Clicking this menu option opens the [Automatic Gully Properties](auto-gully-properties.htm) window, here you can set the minimum spacing, connection angle to the nearest conduit/manhole, and force gullies at low points. You can specify the contributing area and and whether gullies are allowed on a per centreline basis from the [Centreline Properties](centreline-properties.htm) window.

### Set Run Number

When this tool is active you can specify the drainage run number between two manholes. When active select the first and last manhole in the run and a window will appear where you can input the run number. If you drainage network has an outfall with Auto Conduit Numbering turned on then this will override the manual run number and you will get a message telling you that the operation was not possible.

### Add Flow Control

After selecting this menu item you can select a manhole to enter the [manhole flow controls window](manhole-flow-controls.htm). Here you can then add flow controls to a manhole.

### Add Catchpit

This menu item will allow you to pick a manhole and specify the catchpit depth for that manhole. This can also be done in the [manhole properties](manhole-properties.htm).

### Manhole Schedule Properties

This will open the manhole properties window, allowing you to change the display properties for the manhole schedule. See the [Drainage Tools](drainage.htm#manhole_schedule) page for more information about the manhole schedule and these properties.

### Drainable Area Schedule Properties

This will open the drainable area properties window, allowing you to change the display properties for the drainable area schedule. See the [Drainage Tools](drainage-catchment-areas.htm#area_properties_list) page for more information about the drainable area schedule and these properties.

### Renumber Manhole Network

This menu option will open the [Drainage Renumber](drainage-renumbering.htm) window. Here you can specify a naming convention for a drainage network to use. See the [Drainage Renumber help page](drainage-renumbering.htm) for more information.

### Auto Size and Level Network

This menu option will open the [Drainage Auto Size and Level](drainage-auto-sizing-levelling.htm) window.

### Toggle Private Drainage

When this menu item is turned on any new drainage added will be set as private and use the default properties for private drainage.

### Draw Private Drainage

When this menu item is turned off any manholes that are flagged as private in the manhole properties will not be drawn.

### Drainage Display Properties

This menu option will open the [Drainage Properties](drainage-display-properties.htm) window, here you can specify how the manholes and conduits are annotated.

### Set Manhole Group's Phase

After clicking this menu option you draw a region on the plan view around the manholes that you want to modify the phase of, in the bottom left of the view there will be a text box where you can specify the new manhole phase. Right click after you have drawn the region around the section of drainage that you want to modify and select finish to set the new group phase.

### Delete Selected Network

After clicking this menu option you can click on a drainage network to delete the selected network.

### Specify M5-60 Ratio R

This menu option will open the [M5-60 Ratio R](m5_60_ratio_r_map.htm) window where you can automatically get the rainfall values from a map location or manually specify the M5-60 and Ratio R values.

The Tools Menu contains useful functionality for copying data to or from Site3D and finding elements within Site3D.

[Screenshot: tools-menu.png - The Tools Menu](images/tools-menu.png)

### Snap Options

In the Snap Options sub menus you will find the standard set of snap tools as shown in the [Snap Tools](snap-tools.htm) toolbar. For more information see the [Snap Tools help page](snap-tools.htm).

### Measure Line

The Measure Line tool provides a way for you to measure the distance between two points, See the [Toolbox Tools help page](toolbox-tools.htm) for more information.

### Measure Angle

The Measure Angle tool provides a way for you to measure the angle of a line either in respect to the X axis or the angle formed between two connected lines, See the [Toolbox Tools help page](toolbox-tools.htm) for more information.

### Arc Radius

The Arc Radius tool provides a way to quickly determine the radius of arcs, See the [Toolbox Tools help page](toolbox-tools.htm) for more information.

### Entity Properties

This will allow you to click on a design item to see it's properties. For more information see the [Toolbox Tools help page](toolbox-tools.htm).

### Section Design Line

After clicking this menu option select a design line from the plan view to see a simple section of that design line.

### Invert Mouse Wheel

This option allows you to toggle whether the mouse wheel is inverted. By default scrolling the mouse wheel forwards will zoom in, and scrolling backwards will zoom out. Toggling this option will invert this behaviour.

### Zoom Out From Cursor

If this is on then the view will zoom out based on the cursor location when scrolling the mouse wheel. When this option is off the view will be zoomed out relative to the centre of the view.

*Note:* the view will still zoom *in* based on the cursor position when scrolling with the mouse wheel.

### Middle Button Extents

This option will toggle whether double clicking the mouse button sets the zoom to the drawing extents. This will set the view such that the entire drawing is visible.

### 3D View Rotate Relative to Surface

When enabled, the 3D view rotation from horizontal mouse movement will be inverted when the cursor is below the 3D target point and when looking up from below the surface.
This can allow you to rotate the view in a continuous clockwise or counter-clockwise motion and can be thought of as dragging the surface point that was under the mouse cursor.

The standard behaviour with this option disabled is for the 3D view rotation to always rotate the model clockwise when the mouse is moved to the left and counter-clockwise when moving to the right.

### Export 3D Orbit

This option is only available from the 3D view. Clicking this menu option whilst a 3D view is active will bring up the Export 3D Orbit window.

This tool will export a set of images that create the illusion of a 360 degree camera rotation when viewed in succession. A webpage will also be created which uses these images to create an interactive "3D" view where the user can drag the mouse left or right to rotate the view clockwise or anti-clockwise.

### Units

This menu item has a sub-menu which allows you to select between Metric and American/Imperial units.

### Drive on Left/Right

This menu item has a sub-menu which allows you to select between driving on the left or right side of the road.

### Chainage/Stationing

This menu item has a sub-menu which allows you to select between chainage or stationing.

### Country Pack

Clicking this option will open the country picker window. See the [Country Picker help page](country-picker.htm) for more information.

### Misc

The tools in this category are not intended for general use as some of the functionality is experimental.

#### Filter by Colour

Clicking on this will bring up a window which will allow you to select a colour, clicking Okay will then move all the entities with that colour into a separate layer in the [layers window](layers-dlg.htm).

#### Regen Final Surface

Clicking on this will cut the design items into the survey surface. This option doesn't affect the actual survey data or levels. When you move a design item after using this option you will see that there is a hole in the surface in the previous position, select this option again to generate a new final surface.

### Options

Clicking this menu option will open the Options window. See the [Options help page](settings-configuration.htm) for more information.

The plan view tools allow you to change the area that you see in a plan view window by moving the view region or changing the zoom of the view.

The toolbar is positioned at the top of the main window.

[Icon: Plan View Toolbar](images/toolbar-view.png)
  

### [Icon: Zoom In](images/view-zoom-in.png) Zoom In

This tool will zoom the current view in by a small amount when you click whilst it is active.

Scrolling the mouse wheel forward will achieve the same effect in any tool.

This tool can also be executed from the View menu, within the Zoom sub-menu, listed as Zoom In.

### [Icon: Zoom Out](images/view-zoom-out.png) Zoom Out

This tool will zoom the current view out by a small amount when you click whilst it is active.

Scrolling the mouse wheel backwards will achieve the same effect in any tool.

This tool can also be executed from the View menu, within the Zoom sub-menu, listed as Zoom Out.

### [Icon: Zoom to Site Extents](images/view-zoom-extents.png) Zoom to Site Extents

This tool will zoom the current view so that the entire site fits within the current view window.

This tool can also be executed from the View menu, within the Zoom sub-menu, listed as Zoom Extents.

### [Icon: Pan View](images/view-pan.png) Pan View

This tool will allow you to pan the view by holding down the left mouse button and moving the mouse.

Clicking with the middle mouse button and dragging will achieve the same effect in any tool.

This tool can also be executed from the View menu, listed as Pan Realtime.

### [Icon: Zoom to Region](images/view-zoom-region.png) Zoom to Region

This tool will zoom the current view so that the selected region fits within the current view window.

While this tool is active, press and hold the left mouse button, drag the mouse until the highlighted rectangle encompasses the region that you want to view, release the mouse button to zoom the view to the selected region.

This tool can also be executed from the View menu, within the Zoom sub-menu, listed as Zoom Window.

Site3D - Road and Site Design

//<!--
show\_header('Snap Tools');
show\_index();
//-->

The snap tools allow you to position items accurately relative to the drawing, rather than positioning items at the free-hand cursor position.

The snaps toolbar is positioned at the top of the main window.

[Icon: Snap toolbar](images/snap-toolbar.png)

The snap options are available at any time when you are prompted to select a position on the plan-view. For example, when you are placing a centreline point, or when you are moving a manhole.

When a snap option is active you will see a small target square appear on the drawing near to the cursor position. As you move the cursor around the drawing, the target square moves to the nearest appropriate location. The target square indicates the actual position that would be selected.

### [Icon: Snap to point](images/snap-to-point-button.png) Snap To Point

Snap to any point on a visible object on any of the imported sub-models, or on the design model.

For example, *snap to point* will snap to the end of a line or arc, any point on a polyline, a point on the boundary of a hatch, a text insertion point, a centreline IP position, and so on.

### [Icon: Snap to intersection](images/snap-to-intersection.png) Snap To Intersection

Snap to the intersection point of any two line or arc objects which visually cross each other.

*Note*: If the the two lines don't cross, and you want to snap to the point where the extended lines would cross, then first use the [construction line tools](construction-tools.htm) to extend the lines so that do cross.

### [Icon: Snap to line](images/snap-to-line.png) Snap To Line

Snap to the closest point which lies on the line or arc nearest to the cursor position.

*Note*: This can be useful when selecting an offset if an imported layout contains a line indicating the required offset. For example, when using the 'Pick' option for the road width on the [Channels/Kerbs/Footways window](channels.htm).

### [Icon: Snap to perpendicular](images/snap-to-perpendicular.png) Snap To Perpendicular

The snap to perpendicular option works slightly differently to the other snap options. It's operation is as follows:

* Select the Snap To Perpendicular option
* Select a line or arc on the drawing to which you want to be perpendicular.
* Select a point which would lie on the desired perpendicular line.
* The snap target box will now move along the notional line which runs perpendicular to the selected line, through the selected point.

*Note*: This can be useful for placing a perpendicular [construction line](construction-tools.htm).

### [Icon: Snap to arc](images/snap-to-arc.png) Snap To Arc

Snap to the intersection point of the tangent lines at the arc end points.

When using a function that requires identification of an arc, this option can be used to choose one from the drawing. For example, when [adding a new centreline](centreline-new.htm), you can use this option to snap to the arcs of a centreline on an indicative layout, getting both the IP position and the radius at the same time.

### [Icon: Snap to arc centre](images/snap-to-arc-centre.png) Snap To Arc Centre

Snap to the centre-point of the arc closest to the cursor.

### [Icon: Snap to line mid-point](images/snap-to-line-mid.png) Snap To Line Mid-point

Snap to the mid-point of the line closest to the cursor. The mid-point is the point on the line, half-way between the line end points.

### [Icon: Snap to orthogonal](images/snap-orthogonal.png) Snap To Orthogonal

Snap to Orthogonal can be used when specifying the second point of a line. When Snap to Orthogonal is in operation, the target box position will bias towards the exact east - west and north - south directions from the other end of the line. This makes it easy to construct lines at the 90 degree angles on-screen.

Additionally, the north-east - south-west and north-west - south-east directions are biased towards. This makes it easy to construct lines at the 45 degree angles on-screen.

### [Icon: Snap to mid-point](images/snap-to-mid-point.png) Snap To Mid-point

Snap to the mid-point between two other points. When using this snap option, you identify any two points on the drawing, and the position taken is the point mid-way between them.

*Note*: This can be useful for positioning points on a centreline midway between indicative channel lines.

### [Icon: Snap to distance](images/snap-to-distance.png) Snap To Distance

This snap option operates in two different ways which can be chosen from the right-click menu:

[Screenshot: snap-distance-menu.png](images/snap-distance-menu.png)

When *Linear Direction* is active it will prompt you to select a line and then enter a distance. The target box will then be at the given distance extended beyond either end of the line.

When *Linear Direction* is not active it will prompt you to select a point and then enter a distance. The target pox will then appear on a circle of given radius around that point.

### [Icon: Snap to decimal-length](images/snap-to-decimal-length.png) Snap To Decimal Length

Snap To Decimal Length can be used when specifying the second point of a line. When Snap To Decimal Length is active the distance from the previous point is shown next to the cursor, rounded to the nearest decimal place. The target box is shown at the position where the line length would be exactly that shown.

The number of decimal places shown depends on the zoom scale of the drawing, such that when you are working close-in several deimal places of accuracy are shown, and when you're working zoomed-out the rounding is much coarser.

This snap option helps you to easily place lines of an extact length without any construction or measuring required.

### [Icon: Snap to perpendicular lines](images/snap-to-line-perpendiculars.png) Snap To Perpendicualr Lines

Snap To Perpendicualr Lines works in conjunction with the new line commands. After placing at least two points of a line you can then snap along the extensions of that line or snap to perpendicular lines at the placed points.

This snap option helps you to easily create lines that have 90 degree bends or have parallel sections. For example, this helps to create rectangular tanks or ponds.

//<!--
show\_header('Project Auto-Recovery');
